scope: Determine whether the current local PyTorch/TorchTitan DSv4 Flash reference can honestly claim HF one-layer numerical parity.

hf_flash_topology_contract.read: The local HF config artifact captures the Flash dimensions, compress-ratio schedule, indexer shape, and HC fields. Some router and compressor-rope fields may live only in source/config defaults, so source checks remain the stronger topology gate.

hf_inference_block_contract.read: HF publishes an inference-oriented model.py. Its block maintains hc_mult hidden streams via hc_pre/hc_post instead of a plain residual.

local_hyperconnection_primitive_contract.read: A local pure-PyTorch HC primitive exists and passes primitive-level smokes. The separate Flash HC full-layer oracle checks the local attention/MoE wrapper that threads the primitive through a DSv4 block.

local_flash_hyperconnection_layer_oracle.read: The local Flash HC full-layer oracle passes FP32/BF16 MI350 backward smokes at B=1, S=1 with [B,S,4,4096] rank-4 hidden streams.

local_reference_contract.read: The local clean oracle now keeps both the older rank-3 residual block and a rank-4 Hyper-Connection layer wrapper. The HC wrapper is the right local surface for the next HF parity attempt; the rank-3 block remains useful as a non-HC attention/MoE math control.

local_reference_topology_contract.read: The clean local reference is useful for the attention boundary because it has shared WKV, learned compressor classes, and grouped low-rank output. It is still not a complete DSv4 training oracle until Hadamard indexer, router hash/norm, compressor RoPE theta, and MTP coverage are explicit.

torchtitan_wip_contract.read: The current TorchTitan WIP block has an opt-in Hyper-Connection boundary with hc_pre/hc_post around attention and MoE, while the plain residual branch remains available for retained systems canaries.

torchtitan_wip_topology_contract.read: Remaining missing/partial surfaces: shared WKV/proper learned compressor boundary, Hadamard indexer.

ascend_reference_contract.read: The Ascend TorchTitan-NPU reference agrees with HF's HC block contract and already carries MHC converter/autograd wrappers.

ascend_reference_topology_contract.read: The Ascend TorchTitan-NPU reference carries the full source-level Flash topology: shared WKV, learned compressors, Hadamard indexer, grouped low-rank output, compressor RoPE theta, hash-routing first layers, route normalization, and MTP module plumbing.

shared_wkv_attention_boundary.perf_read: Changes attention projection/GEMM and saved-tensor boundary.

learned_compressor_and_indexer.perf_read: Changes LI/compressed-KV forward/backward and activation memory.

hadamard_indexer.perf_read: Small compute, but correctness-relevant for indexer scores.

grouped_low_rank_output_projection.perf_read: Changes attention.wo GEMM shape and adjacent epilogue opportunities.

router_route_norm_and_hash_layers.perf_read: Changes expert load distribution and therefore EP/MoE profiling.

compressor_rope_theta_split.perf_read: Mostly correctness/topology, but it affects sparse-attention inputs.

mtp1_training_head.perf_read: Adds extra loss/head path and backward work for full recipe parity.

topology_gap_report.read: MORI work should continue from the strongest apples-to-apples throughput baseline, but corrected DSv4 performance claims should wait until these topology gaps are either implemented or explicitly scoped out of the metric.

blockers: The configured Transformers tag does not expose a standard deepseek_v4 modeling/configuration module, so there is no drop-in HF training-layer parity class at that version.
next_actions: Use the local Flash HC full-layer oracle as the clean-reference smoke for HF parity work.

Treat reduced layer count as an expected scale reduction, but keep per-layer topology gaps such as hash routing, grouped output, compressor RoPE theta, MTP1, and mHC out of the corrected baseline until implemented or explicitly scoped out.
Keep TorchTitan HC behind TORCHTITAN_DSV4_USE_HYPER_CONNECTIONS until an HC runtime smoke and matched-weight parity are recorded.
Run matched-weight HF-inference-vs-local forward parity with quantization disabled or BF16-only weights.
Only after forward parity is clean, add FP32/BF16 gradient parity against the local HC training oracle.
bottom_line: HF validation is still not persisted. The local clean reference now has a rank-4 HC full-layer smoke, and TorchTitan now has an opt-in HC structural boundary, but matched-weight HF-vs-local parity remains open.
